message:
fix(net): register_interest uses target_interest for fresh registration, not only rearm

The TCP, UDP, and Unix stream register_interest paths had an asymmetry:
when a source was already registered and needed rearm, the combined
target_interest (current | new) was correctly computed and applied. But
when the source was freshly registering for the first time, only the raw
incoming interest flags were passed — ignoring any interest bits that had
already been accumulated on the source prior to the first poll cycle.

This could cause a subtle missed-wakeup if, for example, both a read
and write interest were requested between construction and the first
poll: only the second request's flags would be registered, silently
dropping the first.

Now all three transport register_interest implementations compute
target_interest unconditionally and pass it to both the fresh-register
and the rearm paths, ensuring combined interest is always preserved.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
